# Data-integrity over-cap class: a heal-mode for over-context docs (re-embed loops) ‚Äî split from #14088

## Context

Split from #14088 (expand CorruptionRecoveryGate to drain-stall + over-cap injectors). While V-B-A'ing the detect‚Üíheal mapping for #14088's two classes (`classifyDataIntegrityMode`, `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/dataIntegrityModeClassifier.mjs`), the **over-cap** class surfaced a real design-gap that the drain-stall class does not have ‚Äî so it needs its own design-decision before a gate-injector can assert a *correct* heal.

## The gap

The **over-cap** class = an over-context input that fails to embed (the oversized-doc class). If such a row lands persisted-but-unembedded, `classifyDataIntegrityMode` would classify it as `missingFromVectorCount > 0` ‚Üí the **wal-stall** mode ‚Üí `REEMBED_MISSING`. But **re-embedding an over-context doc re-fails**: `TextEmbeddingService` *refuses* input above the loaded embedding context (#13944, "loaded context below configured embedding context"). So the autonomous heal would **loop** (re-embed ‚Üí refuse ‚Üí still missing ‚Üí re-embed ‚Ä¶) until the rate/anti-thrash bound trips ‚Äî never actually healing.

By contrast, drain-stall (#14088's other class) maps cleanly to wal-stall ‚Üí `REEMBED_MISSING` (the re-embed succeeds), so #14088 can proceed on drain-stall alone.

## Open design questions

1. **Detect-path:** does an over-cap doc land persisted-unembedded (‚Üí wal-stall, the looping case above) or dropped-entirely (‚Üí `count-loss`/`QUARANTINE`, or undetected)? Needs the #13999 incident forensics on the over-cap pathway.
2. **Heal-mode:** re-embed can't fix over-context. Options to weigh ‚Äî (a) **truncate-then-embed** (lossy but recovers a vector); (b) **quarantine the oversized doc** (a new/existing `DataIntegrityTerminal`, no false re-embed); (c) a dedicated over-cap producer that flags the class distinctly so the classifier routes it away from `REEMBED_MISSING`. The classifier has **no over-cap mode today**.

## Why split (not just do it in #14088)

#14088 is a gate-test extension; the over-cap class needs a classifier/actuator *decision* first (likely an ideation), else the gate would certify a non-existent or looping heal. Splitting keeps #14088 shippable on drain-stall and tracks the over-cap design honestly. `needs-design` until the heal-mode is converged.

Refs #14088 (parent gate-expansion), #13999 (incident family), #13944 (over-context refusal), #14240 (the heal-not-escalate cutover), ADR-0026 (autonomous recovery).

Authored by Ada (@neo-opus-ada ¬∑ Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).
